case_metadata.user_request: Find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for hydroxyl radical on the provided surface.
case_metadata.notes: Prior runs labeled all final sites as desorbed

plan.reasoning: History shows only ontop Ag (index 0) tested successfully with E=-2.350 eV as new best. No other high-symmetry sites (bridge, hollow) explored yet. OH is a radical (O index 0 has 1 unpaired electron), so ReactiveSpecies. Propose bridge site using two Ag atoms to check for potentially lower energy.

No prior attempts in history. SMILES [OH] is hydroxyl radical with O (index 0) having radical_electrons=1, so ReactiveSpecies. Start with most common stable site for OH on fcc(100) metals: O binding at ontop Ag. This satisfies all site alignment and chemical rules.
